rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWAN VS. DANIEL LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 24, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT DANIEL LOPEZ' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED with leave to amend in part, OVERRULED in part. Plaintiff's first cause of action, for defamation, implicates the conditional privilege of Civil Code section 47(a) because Defendant's allegedly defamatory statements were posted to internet websites and were intended to warn interested parties not to use Plaintiff's services. As a result, Plaintiff must plead malice, i.e. that Defendant either knew the untruth of his statements or recklessly disregarded the truth when making his statements. (See, e.g., Lesperance v. North American Aviation (1963) 217 Cal.App.2d 336, 341-42.) This variety of malice is not pled in the first amended complaint. Leave is granted to allege such malice, if Plaintiff is able. Plaintiff's second cause of action, for "willful misconduct," appears to be predicated on the violation of various Penal Code sections. It is not clear on what basis Plaintiff believes he can sue in civil court on these claims. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege such a basis, if he can. Plaintiff's third cause of action, termed "negligence per se," is not a cause of action but rather a statutory presumption. (See Das v. Bank of America N.A. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, 738.) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege a negligence cause of action, if he is able. The fourth cause of action, for injunctive relief, includes a request for permanent injunction, which is proper in limited circumstances even in defamation cases. (See Balboa Island Village Inn, Inc. v. Lemen (2007) 40 Cal.4th 1141, 1158.) The demurrer to this cause of action is therefore overruled; one cannot demurrer to less than a full cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWAN VS. DANIEL LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint (Filed Concurrently With Demurrer)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 24, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT DANIEL LOPEZ' Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint. DENIED. Where a supplemental complaint alleging new facts is filed as of right before the defendant answers the complaint, it will be treated as an amended complaint. (Lee v. Bank of America (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 197, 215.) rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MARKHAM VS. SEGWAY OF SF BAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 24, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF BRIAN MARKHAM Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. Granted in part. Motions for leave to amend the pleadings are directed to the sound discretion of the judge. [CCP § 473(a)(1); and see CCP § 576] The Court has discretion to deny leave to amend where a proposed amendment fails to state a valid cause of action or defense. [See California Casualty General Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (1985) 173 CA3d 274, 280-281] Here, Plaintiff states in his moving papers that he requests leave to amend the complaint for three reasons: (1) to submit a written complaint in place of a form Complaint; (2) to eliminate the cause of action for Premises Liability; and (3) to eliminate from the Complaint defendants who have either settled or have been dismissed. The Court grants Plaintiff to amend the Complaint in the above proposed manner. However, P's motion to add the language in proposed motion 27 is denied. P has not requested he be granted leave to amend to add any new claims in the FAC, he has not discussed adding punitive damages in his moving papers, and the proposed FAC fails to state a valid cause of action for punitive damages. In the original Form Complaint Plaintiff did not check the box for punitive damages, and he has not requested leave to amend to add punitive damages. Thus, Plaintiff is not granted leave to amend to add punitive damages. Therefore, any language in the proposed FAC alleging punitive damages is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: ISA J. MUHAWIEH VS. UMPQUA BANK, AN OREGON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 24, 2015, Line 15, DEFENDANT UMPQUA BANK MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Defendant's Umpqua Bank's Motion for Attorney Fees and Expenses is GRANTED IN PART. Defendant filed a Motion for Attorney's Fees on June 1, 2015. "We conclude that in voluntary pretrial dismissal cases, Civil Code section 1717 bars recovery of attorney fees incurred in defending contract claims, but that neither Civil Code section 1717 nor [International Industries, Inc. v. Olen (1978) 21 Cal.3d 218] bars recovery of attorney fees incurred in defending tort or other noncontract claims." (Santisas v. Goodin (1998) 17 Cal. 4th 599, 602.) This court grants six sevenths of defendant's motions for attorney's fees. (Santisas, supra, 17 Cal. 4th at p. 615, "if an action asserts both contract and tort or other noncontract claims, section 1717 applies only to attorney fees incurred to litigate the contract claims.") Six sevenths of Defendant's Motion for Attorney's Fees is $33,396.43. This court grants to Defendant $33,396.43 in attorney's fees. Notwithstanding the above reduction to defendant's attorney's fees incurred to litigate the plaintiff's breach of contract claim, Defendant's attorney's fees are not excessive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ED FRAUENHEIM VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Notice And Subpoena, And For Protective Order Prohibiting Deposition Of Percepta Llcs Person(S) Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 24, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S Motion To Quash Deposition Notice And Subpoena, And For Protective Order Prohibiting Deposition Of Percepta LLCs Person(s) Most Knowledgeable Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT, FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION NOTICE AND SUBPOENA AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER PROHIBITING DEPOSITION OF PEERCEPTA LLC'S PERSONS MOST KNOWLEDGABLE IS DENIED. THERE IS INADEQUATE SHOWING THAT THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY IS UNREASONABLE, OPPRESSIVE OR INTENDED TO HARRASS THE DEFENDANT. TO THE CONTRARY, THE DEPOSITION SOUGHT IS OF A WITNESS WHO ALLEGEDLY WAS DIRECTLY INVOLVED IN THE ACTIONS ABOUT WHICH PLAINTIFF COMPLAINS. AS TO PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH A COPY OF THE FLORIDA SUBPOENA, SUCH FAILURE CAN EASILY BE CORRECTED AND, IN ANY CASE, IS NOT A BASIS FOR ISSUING THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT, NONE WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON AUGUST 24, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)
